---
name: New Anti-Pattern
about: Submit an AI writing pattern not yet in the detection library
title: "[PATTERN] "
labels: anti-pattern, contribution
assignees: ''
---

## Pattern name
Give it a short, descriptive name (e.g., "The Recursive Restatement," "The LinkedIn Loop")

## Category
- [ ] Phrase (specific words/phrases to ban or flag)
- [ ] Structure (document-level or section-level pattern)
- [ ] Cadence (rhythm/sentence-length pattern)
- [ ] Fake depth (pseudo-intellectual pattern)
- [ ] Fake authority (unearned credibility pattern)
- [ ] Genre-specific slop (cliché in a specific domain)

## Pattern description
What does it look like? Be specific enough that someone could detect it.

## Example
Show the pattern in action (2-3 sentences minimum).

## Why it flags as AI
Why does this pattern signal AI generation or lazy writing?

## Detection method
How would you identify this pattern? (Word count threshold, structural signature, frequency rule, etc.)

## Recommended fix
How should it be rewritten? Show a before/after if possible.

## Severity recommendation
- [ ] Hard ban (never use under any circumstances)
- [ ] Soft ban (remove unless it demonstrably earns its place)
- [ ] Earned exception (allowed when intentional and purposeful)

## Where you found it
Did you encounter this in AI output, published writing, detector documentation, or your own editing experience?
